bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages, I am targeting maximum utility and have no reason to concede yet. Since all issues prefer valueC and the most important issueF also strongly favors valueC, the opening bid should anchor at my top outcome across every valid issue.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s all-valueA offer is extremely poor for my preferences, and their “fair/win-win” message is not supported by the numbers, so I will concede only on a low-weight issue for now.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA package that is poor for me. Their “fair/balanced” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their firm bidding behavior, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding my important issues at valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility early-round bid, conceding only on the two lowest-weight issues while protecting the higher-weight issues C, D, E, and especially F. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer three times, so their bids show no real concession despite cooperative language; at t=0.06 there is little time pressure to make large concessions.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08, while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply standing still. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA, which is very low for me and shows no real concession despite their “balanced” messaging, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issues C, D, E, and especially F.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a limited concession on a low-weight issue rather than moving on the most important issueF. The opponent has mostly held at all valueA, with only a small move to valueB on issueD, so I do not treat their “balanced” messages as strong evidence of flexibility yet and will keep valueC on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.12, while conceding further on the low-weight issues issueA and issueB rather than on the more important issueC/issueD/issueE/issueF. The opponent has mostly held at valueA across issues with only a small move to valueB on issueD, so their “balanced” messages are not persuasive enough to justify major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 by conceding only on the two lowest-weight issues, issueA and issueB, while holding firm on the higher-weight issues C, D, E, and especially F. It is still early at t=0.14, and the opponent has mostly held at valueA with only a small concession on issueD, so their “balanced/win-win” messages are not supported by much numerical movement.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making a modest concession on lower-priority issues and one medium-priority issue. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility, though they have shown limited movement on issueD and issueF; I will reciprocate mainly on issueA/issueB and slightly on issueD while holding firm on the highest-value issueF and other important issues.

I am targeting a still-high utility because it is early (t=0.18), but I am continuing to concede on the low-weight issues and one mid-weight issue to test for agreement. The opponent has mostly held at valueA, with limited movement to valueB on issueD and issueF; I will acknowledge their movement on issueD while holding firm on the most important issueF and other high-value issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the same level as my last concession, since time is still early at t=0.20 and there is no need to rush toward the opponent’s low offers. The opponent has mostly held firm at valueA but recently signaled movement on issueC, issueD, and issueF; I will concede on the lower-weight issues A and B and match their latest movement on issueC while holding the more valuable D/E/F at valueC.

At t=0.22 it is still early enough to protect high-value issues, especially issueF and issueE, while showing progress on lower or previously signaled issues. The opponent has made small concessions only on issueD, issueF, and issueC, but their offers remain low for me; I am combining their signaled movement on issueC/issueD while holding firm on my most important issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 while making a visible concession on issueC and the low-weight issues A/B, because we are still early-mid negotiation at t=0.24 and should not over-concede. The opponent has mostly held at valueA but has signaled willingness to move on issueD, including to valueC in the latest offer, so I keep issueD at valueC while holding my most important issueF and issueE at valueC.

At t=0.26 we are still early enough to target a solid utility around 0.78 rather than making a large concession. The opponent has mostly held at valueA but has signaled some flexibility on issueD, issueC, issueE, and issueF; I will hold firm on high-weight issueF and preserve issueD while conceding low-weight issueA/issueB and offering valueB on issueC/issueE as a bridge.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around the early-mid stage, since at t=0.28 there is still time and no need to collapse to the opponent’s low-value packages. The opponent has repeatedly anchored on valueA but has shown some movement on issueD, issueE, and especially issueF, so I am conceding low-weight issueA/issueB while holding firm on high-weight issueF and other important issues where possible.

At t=0.30, I am targeting a still-high utility package while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issues A and B and a moderate concession on E. The opponent’s offers remain quite low for my utility despite small movements on A, D, E, and F, so I should not concede heavily yet; however, matching their latest movement on issueA may help signal flexibility.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around the mid-game rather than dropping to the opponent’s low-utility packages, since their offers have only modestly improved for me and remain far below my prior bids. At t=0.32 I can concede on lower/medium-cost areas by matching their recent movement on issueC and giving valueA on issueA/issueB, while holding firm on high-value issueF and keeping issueD/issueE at valueC. Their messages claim balance, but the actual offer history shows they are still mostly pushing valueA, so I rely on the numbers rather than the rhetoric.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low 0.8s because we are only at t=0.34, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s much lower-value packages yet. The opponent’s offers and messages show repeated pressure for “balanced” deals, but numerically they mostly hold A/B/E low while occasionally moving on C, D, and F; I therefore concede on lower-weight A/B and partially on C/E while holding firm on high-weight issueF and useful issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid-game, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while preserving high value on the more important issueF and several 0.2-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have improved only sporadically and remain low for me, but they have signaled possible flexibility on issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF at different points, so this bid uses those signals without over-conceding at t=0.36.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the high-mid range while making a meaningful concession from my previous offer, since we are in the mid-game but not yet near the deadline. The opponent’s bids and messages show gradual movement on issueC, issueD, and issueF, so I am matching their latest movement on issueC and issueD while holding issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue.

At t=0.40 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a solid but not maximal utility package around 0.78 rather than rushing to accept the opponent’s still-low offers. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim “win-win,” but the actual offers show only modest concessions, most recently moving issueC, issueE, and issueF to valueB; I will concede low-weight issueA/issueB and soften issueD while holding firm on high-value issueF.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 while still protecting issueF, my highest-weight issue, at valueC. The opponent’s latest bid shows real movement to valueC on issueD and issueE, but they still hold issueF at valueA, so I will accept their apparent flexibility on D/E while conceding lower-priority issueA, issueB, and issueC to valueA to make the package more acceptable.

I targeted a still-strong midgame utility by conceding low-weight issueA and issueB while holding high-value outcomes on issueC, issueD, and especially issueF. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s current low-utility package; their offers show intermittent movement on issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF, while their “win-win” messages are cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

At t=0.46 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility by conceding on low-weight issues A and B and also allowing B-level outcomes on some medium issues. The opponent’s offers show repeated willingness to give valueC on issueD and some movement on issueC/issueE, but they still resist issueF, which is my highest-weight issue, so I hold firm there.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.71, conceding further on lower-weight issueA/issueB and moving issueD down to valueB while protecting my highest-weight issueF at valueC. At t=0.48 there is time pressure to make a credible compromise, and the opponent’s recent offers show real movement on issueC, issueD, issueE, and especially issueF despite their repeated “win-win” messaging being cheap talk.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility by holding firm on my highest-weight issueF at valueC while accepting concessions on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.50 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s latest low-utility package; their recent offers show willingness to move on issueB, issueD, issueE, and occasionally issueF, so I am combining those signaled concessions into a more acceptable package.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.74, conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueB and also giving ground on issueC while holding issueF at my top value because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s offers have gradually improved for me, especially by sometimes moving on issueB, issueE, and issueF, but their “win-win” messages are cheap talk and the actual bids still matter more. At t=0.52 there is time pressure to move toward a feasible package, so I am combining concessions they have signaled with protection of my core value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.71, conceding on lower-weight issues and some mid-weight issues while holding issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue and the opponent has previously offered issueF: valueC. At t=0.54 there is some time pressure to move toward signaled opponent positions, and their offers show gradual concessions on issueA, issueB, issueD, issueE, and issueF despite repetitive “win-win” messaging.

I targeted a solid mid-game utility around 0.78 by holding the high-value issueF and issueE at valueC, while conceding the low-weight issueA and issueB to valueA. The opponent’s latest offer moved meaningfully on issueC, issueE, and issueF, but they have also previously offered issueD at valueB, so I am asking for that moderate improvement rather than repeating my prior bid.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility while still making visible concessions on lower-priority issueA and moderate concessions on issueC/issueD. The opponent has gradually moved from all valueA toward some valueB/valueC offers, including valueC on issueB recently, so I am incorporating that while holding firm on my highest-priority issueF and important issueE. Their messages repeatedly claim “win-win,” but I am relying on the offer history rather than the rhetoric.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while making a meaningful concession from my earlier high-value bids: I keep the most important issueF at valueC and preserve issueC/issueE valueC, but concede issueD to valueA and keep low-weight issueA at valueA. The opponent has gradually improved offers to me, especially on issueC, issueE, and issueF, though their “win-win” messages are cheap talk; at t=0.60 I can move toward their signaled positions without dropping to their exact last bid.

I targeted a solid but more conciliatory utility around 0.74, conceding lower-priority issueB and issueD while holding issueF and issueC because they are high-value for me. At t=0.62 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest package outright; their offers show real movement on issueC, issueE, and sometimes issueF despite the repetitive “win-win” messaging. This bid is close to packages they have already signaled, especially around issueC and issueE, while asking them to return to valueC on issueF.

I am targeting a solid mid-late utility by holding firm on the most important issueF while accepting concessions on lower-value areas like issueA and issueE. At t=0.64 there is pressure to move toward packages the opponent has signaled, and their recent offer shows willingness to give valueC on issueC and issueD, while earlier offers show they can sometimes tolerate valueC on issueF. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I am relying mainly on the actual bidding pattern.

I am targeting a utility around 0.78 by holding firm on the highest-weight issueF and seeking valueC on issueC, while conceding issueA and issueD to valueA. At t=0.66 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to drop straight to the opponent’s latest bid; their offers show real concessions on issueB, issueC, issueE, and issueF despite repeated “win-win” messaging, so I am using those revealed moves rather than the rhetoric.

I am targeting a strong but realistic utility around 0.825 by keeping my highest-weight issueF at valueC, preserving issueD at valueC as the opponent just offered, and only asking for a moderate improvement on issueE. At t=0.68 there is meaningful time pressure, so I am moving close to the opponent’s latest package while not accepting their lowest-value issueE. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim balance, but the real signal is their bids: they have recently shown willingness to offer valueC on issueF and issueD and valueB on issueB/issueC, so this counter stays within that concession pattern.

At t=0.70 I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory utility by holding firm on issueF, my most important issue, while moving the lower and mid-weight issues toward valueB. The opponent’s actual offers have gradually improved, including prior willingness to give valueC on issueF, so I am not relying on their repeated “win-win” framing but on the bid history showing some flexibility. This bid avoids repeating my last offer while making a clear concession toward their latest all-valueB structure except on issueF.

I am targeting a solid but not maximal utility package, about 0.825 on my weighted scale, by holding issueF and issueC at valueC while conceding lower-weight issueA to valueA. At t=0.72 there is real time pressure but not yet deadline pressure, and the opponent’s recent offers show movement to valueC on issueC and issueF, so I am using those signals while asking for a modest improvement on issueE from their last bid.

I am targeting a solid utility just above 0.8 by holding firm on issueF, my highest-weight issue, while conceding fully on issueE and issueB to improve acceptability. At t=0.74 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent’s latest offer already gives valueC on issueC and issueD, so I keep those favorable terms and make the smallest key adjustment from their last bid by moving issueF to valueC. Their messages repeatedly claim balance, but I rely on the actual offers, which show gradual concessions across several issues.

I targeted a utility around 0.79 by keeping my highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding substantially on lower or repeatedly contested issues. At t=0.76 there is real time pressure, so I moved close to the opponent’s latest structure, using their offered valueC on issueA and issueF and their valueB on issueC, while asking for moderate valueB movement on issueD and issueE. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim fairness, but the actual offers show gradual concessions with continued resistance on several issues, so I am prioritizing a bid that is near their demonstrated range but still preserves positive value for me.

I am targeting a weighted utility of about 0.78 by accepting most of the opponent’s latest structure while holding firm on issueF, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.78 there is meaningful time pressure, so I am conceding on issueA and issueD and not relying on the opponent’s repeated “win-win” messages beyond what their actual offers show. Their recent bids show they can tolerate issueF: valueC in several packages, so this is a plausible closing compromise.

I am targeting a solid late-game utility around 0.79 by conceding most issues to valueB while holding issueF at valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. At t=0.80 there is meaningful time pressure, but not enough to drop issueF given the opponent has previously offered issueF: valueC several times. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim balance, but the actual offers show gradual concessions with a recent pullback on issueF, so I am countering with a concrete middle package based on the valid domain values.

I am targeting a solid late-stage utility by keeping issueF at valueC, since it is my highest-weight issue, while giving the opponent much of their latest structure on lower or moderate priority issues. At t=0.82 there is real time pressure, so I am conceding on issueE and preserving their favorable recent choices on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD where possible. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim balance, but the offer history is more informative: they have shown willingness to offer valueC on issueB, issueD, and sometimes issueF, so this counter stays close to their latest bid while protecting my main value.

I am targeting a still-positive utility around 0.745, preserving my highest-weight issueF at valueC while conceding low-value issueE and accepting several of the opponent’s latest terms. Time is late but not at the deadline, so I am making a clear concession from my previous offer by moving issueD down to valueB rather than holding at valueC. The opponent’s recent bids show real movement on issueF, issueB, and issueA, but they are still pushing issueD low, so this counter tries to bridge that gap without giving up too much.

I am targeting a still-positive late-stage utility around the mid-0.7 range, conceding more on low/medium issues while preserving issueF as valueC because it is my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s actual offers have gradually included more valueB/valueC concessions despite repetitive “win-win” messaging, and they recently signaled willingness to accept packages with issueB=valueC, issueC=valueB, and issueF=valueC. Since time is late but not at the deadline, I am making a meaningful concession from my prior stance without simply repeating my last bid.
